title: TBM 339: Update and Clarifications
questions: How can I write about my employer or products while avoiding conflicts of interest?
What’s the difference between sponsorship, investing, and paid workshops when disclosing relationships?
How should companies design strategy and planning tooling without creating franken-tools?
Can monetization of a newsletter work while juggling a full-time job?
summary: This post clarifies the author’s relationship to their employer and how they handle disclosure, sponsorship, and investment ties while publishing a newsletter. It also outlines the author’s product philosophy about strategy/portfolio/planning tooling—favoring best-in-class tools and front-line customization over polluted all-in-one systems. The post is aimed at readers who write or create alongside full-time work and want practical guidance on transparency and monetization.

title: Slack Group Invite
questions: Which messaging platform is best for supporting a newsletter community?
How can readers ask questions about TBM posts?
Should a newsletter use Slack or another platform for engagement?
summary: This post invites readers to join a Slack group for questions and general interaction related to a newsletter, including TBM posts. It’s aimed at readers who want a fast support channel and at organizers deciding which messaging platform to use before potentially pivoting later.

title: Supporting the newsletter
questions: How can readers support an independent newsletter?
What subscription pricing options should a creator offer (monthly vs annual)?
Should paid subscribers receive special perks, or is gratitude enough at first?
summary: This post explains a creator’s decision to enable reader support through a paid newsletter subscription. It’s aimed at loyal readers and other independent publishers considering how to monetize writing while continuing to deliver regular content. The post focuses on subscription pricing and the current lack of special perks beyond gratitude.

title: Break
questions: How do I pace my content creation without burning out?
Should I schedule posts in advance to maintain consistency?
What’s a sustainable posting cadence for writing or blogging?
summary: This post is a brief creator update about taking a two-week break after a long streak of frequent writing. It’s for writers and content creators who want ideas on pacing, scheduling, and maintaining sustainable output while protecting against burnout.

title: Image Library Link (and Question)
questions: How should I share a library of drawings and infographics with my newsletter audience?
What is the best way to collect feedback on shared content?
Which tool or workflow can I use to host and share an image gallery for readers to review?
search_terms: how to collect feedback on content
summary: The post is a brief request from a newsletter creator who is experimenting with sharing a library of drawings and infographics. It asks readers to review a shared image gallery and provide feedback, focusing on how to structure the sharing and feedback workflow. This is for creators looking for practical ways to test and improve content distribution using reader input.

title: Thank you for reading TBM in 2020
questions: How should a newsletter creator communicate gratitude and maintain community connection?
What’s a good way to announce upcoming plans for a recurring newsletter?
Should a creator continue a newsletter or pivot to a new format?
search_terms: announcing next year plans for a newsletter
summary: This is a brief creator note thanking readers for engaging with a newsletter during 2020 and encouraging ongoing mutual support. It’s for newsletter audiences and creators who want ideas for a simple community-focused wrap-up and a clear teaser for upcoming plans in the next year.

title: The Beautiful Mess
questions: How can I share new product development posts regularly without overwhelming readers?
What publishing cadence helps me stay motivated while managing limited time?
How do I reduce dependence on platform-specific distribution (like Medium) for my content?
What’s the best way to distribute content across channels (newsletter, RSS, syndication)?
summary: The post outlines an experiment to publish cross-functional product development content more regularly and intentionally by switching to a weekly newsletter format. It’s aimed at creators or product practitioners who struggle with inconsistent posting, platform dependency, and finding a sustainable cadence that keeps readers engaged without overwhelming them.
